title: linaria × cornubiensis
intro: Linaria × cornubiensis, also known as Cornish toadflax or Cornish foxglove, is a rare and endangered biennial plant species native to the southwestern coast of England. The plant's medicinal properties lie in its ability to act as an expectorant, helping to relieve coughs and congestion due to its rich content of alkaloids, particularly cornucucine. Linaria × cornubiensis offers various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and alleviating respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Linaria × cornubiensis typically involve drying the plant's flowers and leaves, which can then be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
